items.headline: City updates youth services master plan and summer safety programs
items.summary: San José reported on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan alongside annual updates on youth empowerment, intervention services, and the Safe Summer Initiative Grant. This reflects the city's ongoing commitment to youth programming and safety initiatives.

items.headline: School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza receives state grant
items.summary: The city council approved a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza, providing funding for cultural and arts programming in the community.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with new consultant agreement
items.summary: The city approved a third amendment to the consultant contract for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, advancing safety improvements at this major intersection.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The city council reviewed Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan, including revised evaluation criteria for bike and pedestrian infrastructure projects. This plan will shape which local streets and paths get safety improvements over the coming years.

items.headline: City Council Considers Changes to Vacancy Appointment Process
items.summary: The council is introducing an ordinance to amend rules around Council vacancies and reduce certain procedures. This could affect how quickly open council seats are filled and who gets appointed.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a major $8.8M senior housing development with 65 apartments at 678 E St John St. This addresses San José's significant shortage of senior housing and represents substantial new affordable-focused housing supply.

items.headline: Trimble Summerhill adds 6-unit housing at Seely Avenue
items.summary: A $2M residential development is adding 6 new units at 0 Seely Av as part of the larger Trimble Summerhill project. This contributes to increasing the city's limited housing stock in an established neighborhood.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition suing city over Mary Avenue safety
items.summary: The city is defending litigation brought by the Garden Gate Coalition regarding Mary Avenue safety concerns. This lawsuit suggests an ongoing dispute about traffic or pedestrian safety measures on that street.
